title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Sharp and Chief Constable Cobbe question Mrs. Haber in her room regarding the death of Patricia Lane. Sharp theorizes that Patricia attempted to call the police from the common room around 6:08 PM but was interrupted when she recognized someone entering her bedroom. Sharp suggests the killer is likely a woman, noting that the perpetrator would have required a level of familiarity to enter the room and might have been an eavesdropper. Mrs. Haber observes that the house walls have poor sound insulation, mentioning that Gene often eavesdrops and that Ginny Tomlinson previously overheard Nigel Chapman telling Patricia that his father had killed his mother. Sharp identifies Ginny, who lives in the adjacent room, and Elizabeth Johnston, who lives on the other side of Patricia's room behind a thin partition, as potential suspects. Sharp also reveals he discovered two hairs caught between Patricia's fingers.

The investigation is interrupted by Archibald Bum, who reports a medical incident involving Elizabeth Johnston. Archibald explains that a week prior, he experienced indigestion and asked Elizabeth for baking soda. Elizabeth informed him that there was some in Patricia's drawer and went upstairs to retrieve it. Archibald took the small bottle and swallowed what he believed to be a spoonful of baking soda, but he subsequently suffered intense stomach pain. After consulting a chemist, Archibald discovered that the substance was not baking soda, but a spoonful of boric acid.
